purpose: The master agent's complete worker prompt construction guide: the full TEMPLATE_VERSION p_track_v2 dispatch template, instruction mode selection (FULL vs LITE), context budgeting (~800 line per-prompt cap, 8000 token estimate), the convention_map system, per-task convention filtering, PKI context injection guidelines, prompt completeness checklist, right-sizing tasks (1-5 min principle), upstream result injection format with KEY DETAILS, and sibling tasks section. The single source of truth for what a worker dispatch looks like.
gotchas: Worker prompts MUST be self-contained — agent must execute without reading additional files or asking questions. Missing context is the #1 cause of worker confusion.
Every worker prompt MUST include both {tracker_root} (for progress reporting) AND {project_root} (for code work) — workers cannot auto-detect these.
PLAN FILE pointer is mandatory: 'PLAN FILE: {tracker_root}/dashboards/{dashboardId}/plan.json' — worker reads its task entry plus context plus depends_on entries from this single artifact.
Prompt bloat is the #1 cause of worker context exhaustion — a 15,000 token prompt consumes 15% of worker context before any code is written. Budget cap: 8000 tokens / ~32KB / ~800 lines.

When a prompt exceeds budget: split the task FIRST (decompose further), then summarize conventions (3-5 most relevant rules as bullets), then trim reference code (specific functions only with line ranges), then condense upstream results (one-line summaries).

FILTER conventions per-worker — never dump full CLAUDE.md. Use the convention_map and Convention Relevance Checklist to select only categories relevant to THIS task. A backend API task gets backend_api/error_handling/naming/types — NOT frontend_styling.
For CLAUDE.md > 500 lines, ALWAYS summarize as 5-10 bullet points — never quote in full. Cap content at ~200 lines.
PKI gotchas are NEVER cut for budget. When trimming: cut conventions first, then relationships, then patterns. Gotchas always stay.

MANDATORY first-write line: 'Write your first progress file IMMEDIATELY with status: "in_progress", stage: "reading_context" BEFORE doing any work.' Without this, workers don't report progress until completion.
TEMPLATE_VERSION must be embedded in the prompt and worker must echo it as 'template_version' field in every progress file write.

INSTRUCTION MODE selection: default to FULL when uncertain. LITE is an optimization for simple tasks — never use it for tasks with dependencies or coordination requirements (3+ files, upstream deps, coordination needs, high deviation risk).

UPSTREAM RESULTS KEY DETAILS field is the most important — bridges WHAT was done with HOW (function signatures, export names, file locations). Without it, downstream worker re-reads files redundantly.

SIBLING TASKS section warns workers about same-wave concurrent tasks: 'You do NOT depend on these tasks ... Do NOT modify any files listed under sibling tasks.' If they discover need to modify, it must be a deviation.
patterns: Full template structure: TEMPLATE_VERSION header -> TASK header -> DESCRIPTION -> CONTEXT (with optional [PKI] relationships) -> PROJECT_ROOT/TRACKER_ROOT/PLAN FILE -> CONVENTIONS (filtered + optional [PKI] file knowledge) -> REFERENCE CODE -> UPSTREAM RESULTS -> SIBLING TASKS -> CRITICAL -> SUCCESS CRITERIA -> FILES (READ/MODIFY/CREATE) -> DIRECTORY -> PREPARATION steps -> LIVE PROGRESS REPORTING (FULL or LITE) -> DEVIATION REPORTING -> EXECUTION RULES -> RETURN FORMAT.
Per-task budget: CONVENTIONS ~200 lines (PKI shares this; cap at 5 files), REFERENCE CODE ~100 lines, UPSTREAM RESULTS ~50 lines per dep, CONTEXT ~150 lines, total ~800 lines / ~8000 tokens.

PKI lookup procedure: check manifest exists -> match task files against manifest.files -> filter out stale -> read annotations via hash -> prioritize MODIFY > READ files (cap 5) -> inject under [PKI] label in CONVENTIONS (gotchas/patterns/conventions) and CONTEXT (relationships).
PKI priority order when budget tight: keep gotchas (1) -> patterns (2) -> relationships (3) -> conventions (4 — first to cut).
Convention map categories: naming, file_structure, imports, frontend_styling, backend_api, error_handling, testing, types — applied per-worker via Convention Relevance Checklist.
Right-sizing principle: each task takes single agent 1-5 minutes; reads 2-3 files / modifies 1-2 files is typical. >10 reads or >5 modifies means decompose further.
UPSTREAM RESULTS injection per dependency: STATUS / SUMMARY (verbatim) / FILES CHANGED (with action) / NEW EXPORTS / DEVIATIONS / KEY DETAILS (1-2 sentences of specific technical context).
RETURN FORMAT: STATUS (completed|failed) / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED (with action) / EXPORTS (omit if none) / DIVERGENT ACTIONS (omit if none) / WARNINGS (omit if none) / ERRORS (omit if none).
Workers also PRODUCE PKI annotations via ANNOTATIONS section in return + annotations field in progress file — master merges these into PKI for next swarm (feedback loop).
conventions: TEMPLATE_VERSION 'p_track_v2' for full mode, 'p_v2' for lightweight.
Quote CLAUDE.md sections directly (not paraphrased) for conventions; for >500 line CLAUDE.md, summarize as bullet points.
Sibling Tasks format: '{sibling_id}: {sibling_title} — modifies {sibling_files}' followed by warning text.
Critical sections never cut for space: SUCCESS CRITERIA, CRITICAL, gotchas (PKI). Cut REFERENCE CODE and CONVENTIONS first.
Append PKI relationships to CONTEXT section under [PKI] label, NOT a separate section.
Worker progress file mandatory writes: (1) before starting work, (2) on every stage transition, (3) on any deviation, (4) on completion/failure.
Stages enum: reading_context | planning | implementing | testing | finalizing | completed | failed.
